entry.title: Re: How to create a list of SAP HANA parameters
entry.content:
Can you please check following SAP Knowledge Base Article:

2186744 - FAQ: SAP HANA Parameters - - > 3. Can parameters be set on different layers?

As an example:

2101244 - FAQ: SAP HANA Multitenant Database Containers (MDC) - - > 19. Is there global allocation limit per tenant?

Also please check:

2600030 - Parameter Recommendations in SAP HANA Environments

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Re: Hello All, Can somebody please let me know how to fin...
entry.content:
RSPCPROCESS: This table stores the individual steps within a process chain, including details such as the process type and the variant. The ABAP program name can be located here, especially if the process type involves an ABAP program.
• VARIANTE: This field stores the variant, which in this case could be the ABAP program name.
• TYPE: This field indicates the type of process (e.g., ‘ABAP’ for an ABAP program).

other tables for process chain 

RSPCCHAIN: This table stores general information about the process chains, including the process chain ID and description.

 RSPCVARIANT: This is the key table where process variants are stored. The variants are the individual steps (like an ABAP program) of a process chain. You can find the variant name here.

entry.title: Re: DTP is getting failed
entry.content: Does the delta data which is new has any columns that can get invalid or junk characters. It could be data issue.

entry.title: Re: What is all about Authorization Server (OAuth) SSO introduced in SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 SP02
entry.content: Could we have an example for Google BigQuery please?  Sorry, new to both of BO and BQ.  Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Talent intelligence hub - 2024 updates and behind the scenes!
entry.content:
Hi @RinkyKarthik, 

We just learned from SAP that migration directly from JDM to TIH is not recommended after all, although is it one of the migration path options. We learned that you should first migrate to JPB and Center of Capablities, modify the Capability Rating Scale in CoC, and then migrate to TIH. When migrating from JDM to TIH, the migration automatically creates and assigns the standard Proficiency Level Scale to migrated competencies, and we can no longer change that (enhancement request exists). This is not mentioned anywhere in SAP documentation or KBA's, but we got confirmation from SAP that this is the way it should have handled. 

Now imagine customers who maybe implemented Capability Portfolio back in 2021 when it was introduced. Customers tried it out, and realized it is not that flexible since the limitation of rating scale not to be changeable. Customers could not know that any decision about Capability Ratins Scale made back then would be set to stone also when migrating to Talent Intelligence Hub, which did not even exist in 2021 yet. And now customers are stuck with the decision made with Capability Portfolio, since we cannot change the rating scales or their assignment to attribute types. 

How on earth are we ever going to be able to implement TIH as we want to due to all these limitations and unexpected and undocumented issues? Please make changing the rating scale possible, otherwise customers will never start adopting Talent Intelligence Hub. 

BR,
Marika

entry.title: ATP is not working when MRP area is deactivated
entry.content:
Hi All,

ATP is not working in sales order when the MRP area is deactivated, which was active in the system previously.

Kindly let me know if any solution for the ATP to work without MRP area.

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Sydney May '24
entry.content:
Hi @Mariss excited to meet you too 😊

Great you are interested to set up a casual event like Stammtisch in other parts of Australia as well. It's really a great opportunity to build up a local community or to strengthen the already existing one. Please have a look to this page, how to become an event organizer. Please follow the steps there, and my colleagues will get back to you as soon as possible.

Looking forward to seeing more events in Australia!

entry.title: Re: Load sample data on demo tenant for SAP Sales Cloud V...
entry.content:
Hello @mgbernardo,

As this is a demo system, you can request SAP support to load sample data via an SAP ticket.

Thanks and Regards,
Kunal Bansal
SAP CX Solution Architect

entry.title: Re: how to track users accessing fiori
entry.content: The standard solution using SAP Cloud ALM is Real User Monitoring. Unfortunately right now no content for Build Work Zone is availalbe. But you could start with the content for Cloud Foundry and standalone UI5 apps.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David,

I hope you're doing well.

I wanted to inform you that I've recently posted on the blog and have also registered on Digital Skills. However, I haven't yet received the campaign code.

Could you please look into this and ensure the code is delivered at your earliest convenience?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I'm an SAP ABAP Developer S/4 HANA from Pakistan. I've been working in SAP fields for 1 Year.
I identified as a person for this certification because I am a woman and first generation university graduate in my family.
I really love working with SAP. I want to keep learning and growing in this field for lifetime to have a great career opportunity.
I'm eager to take a free certification attempt because I believe it would help me to learn more knowledge in SAP and Career Opportunities.
I hope you will consider my application for the free certification in SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud.
Thanks a Lot for this grateful Opportunity!

entry.title: Re: Data Binding with Repeating Items and Complex Tables in Print Forms
entry.content:
Hi @Felix_Wyskocil 

Thanks for reply.

I managed this issue by get rid of all Table on Adobe Form and replace it with Subform.

I think it was because i use Table that's causing the next page on item rows get overlapped following header's width column. 

Tips to build complex layout design form with many variations item rows width is not to use Table. Use Subforms.

entry.title: System Error While running simulation of document splitting Migration
entry.content:
During Document Splitting Migration, while executing "Simulate Document Splitting for Journal Entries" with Tcode "FINS_SIS_SIM_SPL", following error is appearing:

 

System error (termination point BALANCE_CCODE_SMART)

Message No. GLT0000

Diagnosis

An internal error has occurred.

System Response

You cannot continue with processing.

Procedure

Contact SAP to establish whether the error can only be rectified by a
program correction, or whether it can also be rectified by changes to
the settings. There may also be an error in the updating of the data
which must be resolved.

Specify the following parameters when reporting the error:

o Message : GLT0/000

o Program : SAPLGLT0

o Termination point : BALANCE_CCODE_SMART

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting of Performance Issues Between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Ali,

for me that link leads to nowhere. Can you please check and confirm that this is the correct link?

entry.title: Re: What is the use of Retention Period for Scores
entry.content:
Hello Amshu,
Let's consider you have set monthly calculation and retnetion days as 2days.

  1. Now, if you set score-calculation-date as 1st of month, it'll calculate the score on 1st.
  2. And the calcualted score will get expired on 3rd of month and for rest of 27 days, you'll not be able to use that expired-score.

So you can set these parameters accordingly.

Kunal

entry.title: Re: SAP TechEd Bangalore 2023: Data & Analytics highlights

entry.title: Re: Change Payment Terms in Preliminary Billing Document...
entry.content:
The Payment terms can be set in sales order and the same payment terms are being carried to the preliminary Billing document. 
This cannot be changed as of now in public cloud in the preliminary document separately.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud studio - Unattended Bot - Execution after S...
entry.content: Are you getting stuck during the selection of SAP Systems? And are you using Chrome? If so, try switching to Edge, close all Chrome windows, and test it again. I encountered this issue in design mode.

entry.title: Is there a possibility to show description to input parameters in DATASPHERE ?
entry.content:
Dear Community Members,

In SAP Datasphere we can assign input parameters to a dimension so we get to choose the value rather than having a free entry field.

Is there a wasy to get a description next to the value selected?As an example: I have a table with a key and a description. The key is the value to be selected (as input parameter)but the description in the description column of the table should also be shown for clear understanding.

 

 

regards

sagar

entry.title: Upcoming Deprecation of VBScript, its Impact on SAP GUI Scripting and Resulting Strategy
entry.content:
SAP Note 3484031 describes a few consequences, for the next release 8.10 of SAP GUI for Windows, of the future deprecation of VBScript by Microsoft. The note refers to the post Deprecated features for Windows client from Microsoft. The post by Naveen Shankar Chilla with the title VBScript deprecation: Timelines and next steps gives us more details. One very interesting part here is the deprecation plan with the deprecation timeline. The SAP note tells to us that we should approach this topic calmly, and that is the right thing to do. It will be a long time before VBScript is finally no longer available in Windows - if any. Nevertheless, we need to take a look so that this doesn't come as a surprise to us.

JScript

The SAP note describes that it is planned to extend the SAP GUI Scripting with the alternative language JScript (may be subject to change), which is also used in the SAP GUI for Java. As a first step I think this is a good consequence and I hope that the SAP GUI Scripting Recorder will be extended actually in the next release. It would be really great to have another alternative scripting language in the standard.

PowerShell

Furthermore the SAP Note tells that additional scripting languages, e.g. like PowerShell, are not planned to be supported at the moment. We will see what the future brings. JScript bases also on the Windows Script Host, as VBScript, something similar could be expected in a distant future. But that is just my personal assumption. In my opinion an extension of the SAP GUI Scripting recorder with PowerShell would be a real benefit.

Microsoft recommends in the post Features removed or no longer developed starting with Windows Server 2025 (preview) to use PowerShell as a replacement for VBScript for automating tasks, custom actions, or scripts. I know it is for server, but let us take this as an indication of the destination of this journey for the clients. I also think it makes more strategic sense to use PowerShell.

Conclusion

To summarize, we can say that the scripting language environment of Windows changes, slowly but surely. SAP GUI Scripting is not unaffected of this. Many business processes were automated with SAP GUI Scripting and VBScript, so a long-term adjustment should be made. A strategy should be defined to ensure that all automation scripts are converted to a new format, in the case of a change. When automated processes that you rely on suddenly stop working, hectic activities normally arise. This can be avoided. Even if the remaining period still seems long, this requirement should not be forgotten. Because these kind of business process automation in particular are often realized by the subject matter experts and not by the IT department.

entry.title: Re: Version of ERP capable of connecting with Integration Suite
entry.content: i think it's depend of the scenario or the way of the implementation you choose. If you just want to connect to the system via Cloud Connector the ERP can be quit old.

entry.title: Configuration dumping while saving the PO CUIB1 X699
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am getting the following error when i am trying to save my purchase order.

We are using plant-based configuration and when i am trying to change the plant after saving the PO it is dumping with the error message CUIB1 X699.

Runtime Errors MESSAGE_TYPE_X

Date and Time 

Short text

The current application triggered a termination with a short dump.

What happened?

The current application program detected a situation which really

should not occur. Therefore, a termination with a short dump was

triggered on purpose by the key word MESSAGE (type X).

Error analysis

Short text of error message:

Duplicate owners: Contact LO-VC development

Long text of error message:

Technical information about the message:

Message class....... "CUIB1"

Number.............. 699

Variable 1.......... " "

Variable 2.......... " "

Variable 3.......... " "

Variable 4.......... " "

Trigger Location of Runtime Error

Program CL_CBASE_SPECIAL_ERP==========CP

Include CL_CBASE_SPECIAL_ERP==========CM006

Row 51

Module type (METHOD)

Module Name IF_CBASE_CHECK~CHECK_OWNER

ce Code Extract

SourceCde

1 it_owner = lt_owner

2 IMPORTING

3 et_instance_owner = lt_inst_owner ).

4

5 LOOP AT lt_inst_owner REFERENCE INTO ls_inst_owner

6 WHERE instance <> iv_root_instance

7 AND del_flag IS INITIAL.

8

9 TRY.

0 * check configuration is marked for deletion

1 cl_cbase=>get_current_cbase_by_instance(

2 EXPORTING

3 iv_instance = ls_inst_owner->instance

4 iv_check_only_buffer = ggc_true

5 IMPORTING

6 eo_cbase = lo_cbase ).

7

8 * no deletion

9 CHECK lo_cbase->marked_for_deletion( ) IS INITIAL.

0 * no owner change

lo_cbase->get_owner_of_cfg(

EXPORTING

iv_instance = ls_inst_owner->instance

IMPORTING

es_owner = ls_other_owner ).

CHECK ls_other_owner = is_owner.

CATCH cx_cbase_error . "#EC NO_HANDLER

ENDTRY.

MESSAGE x699(cuib1).

ENDLOOP.

CATCH cx_cbase_error . "#EC NO_HANDLER

ENDTRY.

ENDMETHOD.

Kindly help me out to find a possible solution for this as i have very less idea to solve this.

Regards,
Aparna

entry.title: For On account Payments or Receipts Transaction. Profit center must be Mandatory.
entry.content:
Dear Experts

There is a specific Requirement from customer that For On account Supplier Payments or Customer Receipts transactions. Profit center should be mandatory if not it should throw an error message.

We created a validation rule for profit center field should not be empty while posting the On account payment/receipt transaction postings.

validation rule for the Bank GL account or reconciliation GL account. we cannot make mandatory for reconciliation because we don't have a field to feed profit center while booking an invoice booking.

In the same way we cannot have validation rule by using Bank GL account also.

Help us to resolve this issue.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: purchasing groups value limits
entry.content:
No. Not with applications settings or customizing.

But have a look to PO BadI's MM_PUR_S4_PO_* if one of them allows you to do what you want. Means you have to define your logic and tables with settings and at the end the BAdI should check your PO. Purchasing group at PO is on header level, means your restrict value is for whole PO spend.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Dump MEMORY_NO_MORE_PAGING
entry.content: Dear Rahul, Did you find the solution ?

entry.title: Re: ATP is not working when MRP area is deactivated
entry.content:
Hello @sreejith_gkn 

As per SAP Help page Implementing MRP Areas:

On top of that, MRP areas are always on S/4HANA.

So if you really have a situation as you said:

the MRP area is deactivated, which was active in the system previously

It means that the system was somehow forced to deactivate MRP areas. That might have caused data inconsistency that you experience as ATP not working.

I don't see any other explanation as whether ATP works or not really doesn't depend on MRP areas being active or not.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Custom CDS View Through Eclipse While Deprecated i am getting Error in Public Cloud System

I have Created One Custom CDS View through Eclipse and Released the Task to Customizing tenant.

Now i want to Deprecated status back to Normal, I am trying to do that Deprecated but i am getting Error.

 

This is the Error i am getting. So please suggest if i missed something to do.

entry.title: Re: GR and IR Value as per Vendor Wise not Purchase Order...
entry.content:
Hello @Rabbani1 

I guess you need that data for you own development. So if you have required values by purchase order number, what is the problem to summarize them by vendor number? Vendor numbers can be easily derived from purchase numbers.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: missing icon in VA05
entry.content: Please raise an Incident with SAP

entry.title: Re: Variant Configuration - Object Dependencies - CEIL Function not Working
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

I have just read that there is some bug with the CEIL function.
Solved: Alternative for CEIL Function, because does not wo... - SAP Community


As an alternative you can try to use the ROUND function.

$self.TC_BOM_QTY = ROUND ( MDATA $self.TC_BOM_QTY / TC_PC_PER_BOARD, 0, ROUND_CEILING )

entry.title: Re: Why is doing batch without stock while doing POGI?
entry.content:
Hello @mdgolamrabbani 

What you have shown on the 2nd screenshot is batch search help, actually one of them, as SAP provides numerous search helps here:

If you don't find a search help that works for you, you can always build a new one - see SAP Help Search Helps

I think you requirement is not entirely correct. You should batch determination strategy to get batches for PGI, not a manual search help. Search help is to find a batch master record, not batch stock that you need for PGI.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Hi Experts, the stocks are present in Valuated GR blocked Stock, we need to clear (make it zero)
entry.content:
The stocks are currently present in valuated GR Blocked stock, this is because of the GR movement Type 107.
I understand that reversal of GR with movement type 109 is a possible solution, but we have many partial GR from the year 2017, which cumulatively made bigger quantity.
So, we are expecting a kind of solution in which with the material number, plant and quantity in Valuated GR blocked Stock can be given as input.
we need to clear (make it Zero) the stock balance in the Valuated GR blocked stock

entry.title: Re: setFilename dynamically in TPM for AS2 configuration
entry.content:
Hi @AlexanderApel ,

Is there any way to incorporate adding of the AS2filename in the TPM configuration itself, rather than using a content modifier and groovy script?

If using a content modifier and groovy then where to add those, as in which step of the custom integration flow should we add those?

Thanks,

Radhika

entry.title: Re: Problem to get the missing parts
entry.content: What report / transaction is that?

entry.title: How to estimate the capacity unit with Commerce Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator
entry.content:
With the introduction to SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits, SAP Commerce Cloud non-production environments can be scaled up or scaled down as a self-service operation on SAP Commerce Cloud Portal. Non-production environments can be scaled up during certain periods of time for various purposes, including performance testing. This self-service scaling capability is available to customers who purchase SAP Commerce Cloud consumption credits. 

SAP is planning to update the Capacity Unit Estimator in a few months with improvements. This article will be updated accordingly when the new estimator becomes available.

 

Commerce Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator parameters and results

The estimator tool has various parameters. The estimated units appear based on the parameters used on the top left of the page.

SAP Commerce Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator: https://commerce-cloud-cu-estimator.cfapps.ca10.hana.ondemand.com/

Display Range: Displays the estimated units per hour, per day, per month, or per year. Select the display range based on your use case.

(Fig. 1. Display Range)

If the use case is to scale up the environment for a 2-week performance testing period once in a quarter, select Daily to see daily estimation. Multiply the results by 14 days, then multiply by 4 to view the annual estimation. If the use case is to permanently scale up, select Yearly to find the annual estimation.

The result appears on the top right Estimated Units panel.

Estimated Units: Displays the results based on the parameters from the estimator tool. 

  • Database: Estimated Units from database parameters Performance and Size.
  • Compute: Estimated Units from Estimated VM Count, Solr Managed Disk Storage, and Zookeeper Managed Disk Storage. Estimated VM Count are calculated based on the parameters from each service.
  • Total CU: Overall estimated capacity units in the specified display range.
  • CU Entitlement: Capacity Units entitlement to the environment selected in Environment Size parameter. Each non-production environment customers own has defined Cu entitlement per non-production environment type.
  • Additional CU: Estimated additional capacity units for the environment use case in the selected Environment Size.

(Fig. 2. Estimated Units)

Environment Size: Selector for the non-production environment to scale. The selection of an Environment Size provides the CU Entitlement for that environment with no scale up/down events. The CU Entitlement is used to estimate how many additional CUs are required according to the desired setup.

(Fig. 3. Environment Size)

Select the right environment type from the pull down for the scaling use case. If the use case is to scale up from Staging environment, select Staging / Medium sandbox. 

Database: Selectors for Azure SQL database DTU type and storage size. 

(Fig. 4. Database)

Compute > Virtual Machine: VM Type selector for the non-production environment.

(Fig. 5. Virtual Machine)

Different VM Type can be used in each non-production environment. This parameter should be used once all parameters for services are filled. When VM Type is changed, the Estimated VM Count changes. The estimated VM Count is used for the capacity unit estimation. Please select the VM Type carefully as the actual VM count may vary.

Services (Backoffice, API, Storefront, Background Processing, JS Storefront, JS SSR Container, Apache Ingress, Solr, Zookeeper): Specify the right parameters for each service. 

These services replicas run on deployed VM machine. Input the estimated scaled environment sizing for each service.

  • Number of Replicas: Node counts for the service.
  • Request/Limit CPU: CPU count for the service.
  • Maximum Java Memory (Gi): Java memory allocated for the service in Gigabyte.
  • Memory Scale Factor: Java memory scaling factor for each service.
  • Total Container Request/Limit Memory (Gi): Read only calculated result from Maximum Java Memory and Memory Scale Factor.
  • Managed Disk Storage: Disk storage type for Solr and Zookeeper service.

Estimating the current non-production environment capacity units

Now that you are familiar with the estimator. Use the estimator to calculate the estimated capacity unit consumptions for your current non-production environment.

Sign in to the Cloud Portal for the SAP Commerce Cloud, open one of your non-production environments. The screenshot below is d1 Development example.

(Fig. 6. Environment View)

You can find the environment setup in the following areas on the Environment page on the Cloud Portal.

  1. Scaling Configuration
  2. Scaling Tab of each service

Additional information can also be found in Dynatrace for the environment. Please see below for more details where to find the configurations in the Cloud Portal.

Environment > Scaling Configuration: Displays database DTU, database storage, VM Type and maximum number of VMs.

(Fig. 7. Scaling Configuration)

You can use this information in the estimator tool to figure out the estimated capacity unit for the database. You can also use VM Type for Virtual Machine if the environment has different VM Types than the default selections in the estimator tool.

(Fig. 8.  Mappings between Cloud Portal information and Capacity Units Estimator for DB and VM)

Services > Scaling Tab: Displays Number of Replicas, CPU Limit, Request / Limit CPU, Maximum Java Memory (Gi), and Memory Scale Factor in each service. 

(Fig. 9. Service Scaling information)

 (Fig. 9. Service Scaling information)

Please follow the steps below to locate the Scaling tab described above.

  1. Open a non-product environment in the Cloud Portal.
  2. Locate Services in the page, click View all.

    (Fig. 10. Services section in Cloud Portal)
  3. All services are shown as follows in a table format. Click API as an example to view details for the service.

    (Fig. 11. Services View)
  4. Click Scaling tab as seen in the Fig. 9. above.

You can use these information in the estimator tool. The following image is a mapping example of a Backoffice service.

 

(Fig. 12. Mappings between Cloud Portal information and Capacity Units Estimator for services)

Repeat the same exercise for all services from Backoffice, API, Storefront, Background Processing, JS Storefront, JS SSR Container, Apache Ingress, Solr and Zookeeper in the non-production environment.

Once you repeat the exercise for all services, the estimator tool should have equivalent setup as in the non-production environment you are working on, except for Solr and Zookeeper Managed Disk Storage.

This information is not available in Cloud Portal or Dynatrace. Open a service ticket and ask SAP Customer Support to provide the current setup for each non-production environment. Alternatively, you can use the following Managed Disk Storage properties, which are generally used as a default in each environment.

 

Environment SizeSolr Managed Disk StorageZookeeper Managed Disk Storage
Development / Small SandboxStandard E3 LRS 16GBStandard E3 LRS 16GB
Staging / Medium SandboxPremium P3 LRS 16GBPremium P3 LRS 16GB
Large SandboxPremium P3 LRS 16GBPremium P3 LRS 16GB
X-Large SandboxPremium P3 LRS 16GBPremium P3 LRS 16GB

(Fig. 13. Standard Managed Disk Storage for Solr and Zookeeper)

When you fill all information in the estimator, the estimator displays the estimated units of the environment.

Before finalizing your exercise, review the following Dynatrace view.

Dynatrace > Infrastructure Observability > Hosts: Displays number of VMs deployed in the non-production environment.

(Fig. 14. Hosts view)

You can find the actual number of VMs deployed in the environment. Make sure to check the Estimated VM Count in the estimator tool.

If the actual number VMs deployed in the environment is higher than the Estimated VM Count in the estimator tool, adjust some parameters under Compute to bring the Estimated VM Count match with the actual VM Count in Dynatrace. This adjustment can provide a more accurate CU estimate.

When you complete the estimator exercise, it is recommended to take a screenshot of the estimator as the tool currently does not support save or export functions.

Repeat this exercise for all other non-production environments to estimate the capacity units for each non-production environment.

Calculate total Capacity Units required for one Subscription

Consumption credits are meant to be pooled within an entitlement set. An entitlement set refers to the collection of all environments within one subscription. That is, within one subscription/entitlement set, a customer may have several sandboxes of different sizes (e.g. 1 small, 1 medium, 1 large, and 2 XL sandbox). The estimation of CUs with the calculator is done for a single environment at the time. This single estimation will give you insights into how many CUs are needed to scale the sandbox in question. However, the estimator does not provide the source of the CUs needed for the desired scaling. You may choose to buy extra CUs for the scaling if you have no other source of CUs. If you could downscale one of your sandboxes for a period of time, this will save you CUs, which could be used for scaling desired non-production environment.

Example:

Entitlement set contains:

  • 1 small sandbox (yearly entitlement of 46,492 CUs)
  • 1 large sandbox (yearly entitlement of 151,164 CUs)
  • 1 XL sandbox (yearly entitlement of 578,830 CUs)
  • Total yearly entitlement: 46,492 + 151,164 + 578,830 = 776,486 CUs

Desired outcome:

  • Small sandbox upscale to a large sandbox for a period of a year (104,672 CUs need)
  • Large sandbox remains as is for a period of a year
  • XL sandbox can be downscaled for a period of six months (289,415 CUs saved)

This means that the 289,415 CUs saved in the downscaling of the XL sandbox, can be used to upscale the small sandbox into a large sandbox. No purchase of extra CUs would be necessary under the described scenario. As such, it is important to always take into consideration all environments within one entitlement set to calculate the total needs of CUs for a yearly period.

Estimating the capacity unit for example use cases

You can use the estimator to find the estimated capacity units for your use case from an existing non-production environment.

First, you must develop the scale up use cases for each environment.

Examples:

  • Scenario 1: Permanently scale up Azure DTU for Development environment to Standard 200 DTU.
  • Scenario 2: Temporally scale up Staging environment for 2-week performance testing every quarter.

IMPORTANT: Start from the current non-production setup. The estimator default parameter is set to basic Development / Small sandbox setup, which may not be the same as what the current non-production environment has.

Example Scenario 1: Permanently scale up Azure DTU for Development environment to Standard 200 DTU.

Steps

  1. Follow the previous “Estimating the current non-production environment capacity units” exercise to set the baseline from the Development non-production environment.
  2. Take a screenshot of the estimator. This prevents from an unexpected parameter reset as the estimator currently does not support export or save functions.
  3. Change Database > Performance parameter to Standard S4 200 DTU.

    (Fig. 15. Database)
  4. (Option) Change Database > Size if you need to increase the size of the database.
  5. Review Estimated Units. You can find 445 monthly additional capacity units are estimated for this use case.

    (Fig. 16. Estimated Units)

Example Scenario 2: Temporally scale up Staging environment for 2-week performance testing every quarter.

Steps

  1. Figure out the performance testing scaled up configuration. If the plan is to use the production like setup, follow the instructions in the “Estimating the current non-production environment capacity units” section" for the production environment.
  2. Take a screenshot of the estimator . This prevents from an unexpected parameter reset as the estimator currently does not support export or save functions.
  3. Change Display Rage to Daily

    (Fig. 17. Display Range)
  4. Change all parameters to the performance test scaled up setup.
  5. (Option) Adjust Virtual Machine Type, review the Estimated VM Count, and Additional CU. 

    Default Virtual Machine Type is Standard_D8ads_v5 (8 vCPU/32 Gi). Selecting a bigger VM box, i.e. Standard_D16ads_v5 (16vCPU/64 Gi), may give the better estimated CU as it may reduce number of VMs depending on the scaled use case, especially with high scaled setup such as the performance testing scenario.

    Review Estimated VM Count and Additional CU to see the reasonable additional CU setup.

    Please note, the automation engine may deploy a greater number of Virtual Machine than the estimated count due to other overhead. Please take potential increase of additional VM when deciding the Virtual Machine Type.

  6. Finalize the Virtual Machine Type.
  7. Review Estimated Units. You can find 273.4 daily additional capacity units are estimated for this use case.

    (Fig. 18. Daily Estimated Units)
  8. Multiply Daily Estimated Units 273.4 by 14 days. Additional 3,827.6 CUs are estimated for the 2-week temporally performance testing. Multiply 3,827.6 CUs by 4 gives estimated additional CUs for perfomance testing every quarter for a full year.

Summary

Consumption credits are the new concept for SAP Commerce Cloud to allow customers self-service scaling for non-production environments. These credits are pooled for entire non-production environments under one subscription which can be used for scaling up or scaling down each non-production environment independently. 

We hope this new concept along with the estimator tool helps all SAP Commerce Cloud customers drive business faster.

Contacts

Please comment in this blog or send an email to following contacts.

  • Axel Grzymisch axel.grzymisch@sap.com 
  • David Hickey david.hickey@sap.com 
  • Taka Kitazume takahiro.kitazume@sap.com 

References

  • Commerce Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator
  • Commerce Cloud Technical and Functional Specifications
  • Estimating Required Capacity Units 
  • Consumption Credits
  • Your Key to Agile Scaling! SAP Commerce Cloud, Consumption Credits

entry.title: Re: Variant Configuration - Object Dependencies - CEIL Fu...
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

I have just read that there is some bug with the CEIL function.
Solved: Alternative for CEIL Function, because does not wo... - SAP Community


As an alternative you can try to use the ROUND function.

$self.TC_BOM_QTY = ROUND ( MDATA $self.TC_BOM_QTY / TC_PC_PER_BOARD, 0, ROUND_CEILING )

entry.title: SAP GUI HTML / Browser control use F12 Development tools
entry.content:
How to get the F12 browser dev tools open for SAP GUI HTML Browser control?

Works for SAP GUI HTML / Browser control via Edge (based on Chromium) so WebView2. 

Create these Win Registry Keys either manual

Path:  Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\WebView2\AdditionalBrowserArguments

Name: saplogon.exe
Value "--auto-open-devtools-for-tabs"

or via admin powershell

 

New-Item 'HKCU:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\WebView2\AdditionalBrowserArguments' -Force | `
 New-ItemProperty -Name saplogon.exe -Value "--auto-open-devtools-for-tabs" -Force

 

 

 

I needed it to debug some cookie issue.

btw the user dir and cookies folder is here:

"C:\Users\tsoadmin\AppData\Local\SAP\SAP GUI\EdgeUserData\EdgeUserDataSystemAware\EBWebView\Default\Network\Cookies" (SQL lite DB) but chrome has exclusive lock on the file so can't be read while open.

Here the full cmd of a running WebView2 instance.

 

"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\EdgeWebView\Application\127.0.2651.105\msedgewebview2.exe" --embedded-browser-webview=1 --webview-exe-name=saplogon.exe --webview-exe-version="800 Final Release - 64bit" --user-data-dir="C:\Users\tsoadmin\AppData\Local\SAP\SAP GUI\EdgeUserData\EdgeUserDataSystemAware\EBWebView" --noerrdialogs --embedded-browser-webview-dpi-awareness=1 --edge-webview-custom-scheme=sap-cust,1,1,sapevent,1,1,saphtmlp,1,1,sapr3,1,1 --enable-features=MojoIpcz,msSingleSignOnOSForPrimaryAccountIsShared --mojo-named-platform-channel-pipe=9484.10484.18364521422129778599

"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\EdgeWebView\Application\127.0.2651.105\msedgewebview2.exe" --type=renderer --noerrdialogs --user-data-dir="C:\Users\tsoadmin\AppData\Local\SAP\SAP GUI\EdgeUserData\EdgeUserDataSystemAware\EBWebView" --webview-exe-name=saplogon.exe --webview-exe-version="800 Final Release - 64bit" --embedded-browser-webview=1 --embedded-browser-webview-dpi-awareness=1 --edge-webview-custom-scheme=sap-cust,1,1,sapevent,1,1,saphtmlp,1,1,sapr3,1,1 --video-capture-use-gpu-memory-buffer --lang=en-US --device-scale-factor=1 --num-raster-threads=2 --enable-main-frame-before-activation --renderer-client-id=5 --js-flags="--harmony-weak-refs-with-cleanup-some --expose-gc --ms-user-locale=" --time-ticks-at-unix-epoch=-1723655821980318 --launch-time-ticks=529510535327 --field-trial-handle=3568,i,14546398177687338828,8625289174098165138,262144 --enable-features=MojoIpcz,msSingleSignOnOSForPrimaryAccountIsShared --variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=3580 /prefetch:1

 

 

here another method [via utility called IEChooser.exe ]

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/obscure-productivity-tips-debug-javascript-running-within-sapgui-browser/ba-p/13390663

Example how it looks (for content server document viewer with HTML Control)

example of F12 debug

entry.title: Looking for the best way to calculate inflation
entry.content:
I am working on a project where a forecast is calculated via a data action, this forecast value should now be part of the planning. The planning looks like this, for example:

I have a value on an account "Acc_1", this value is on the source "Forecast". Now I want to enter a value on the same account "Acc_1", but this value should be on source "Adjustment". A % value is entered on
 the account "Acc_2", this is on the source "Inflation". 

I want to write a DataAction that calculates the following:

Acc_1(Forecast) = Acc_1(Forecast) + (Acc_1(Forecast) * Acc_2(Inflation)) + Acc_1(Adjustment).

Or would it be a better way to create new accounts that have the respective ending like "_inflation".
Without including the Source?

One consideration was to create new accounts and work with account formulas. To do this, I would have created new accounts, provided them with the extension _x and written the formula in Acc_1_Forecast that calculates everything. But since a data action is supposed to calculate Acc_1_Forecast, I can't write a formula here.

So I'm looking for the best way to solve this problem.

But currently the attachment service is based on the DMS technology , 
Once a purchase order is enabled for the HDM system then dropbox facility . Please do raise a request using the customer influence portal

entry.title: Re: Is SAP and Sustainability a Myth ?
entry.content:
Sadly, despite genuine and well intended efforts, it is a myth. The predominant business goal is growth, which is the logic of a cancer not sustainability - see Sustainable growth? for straightforward explanation with numbers.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: No of Same Work Center

Hello @raviram08 

To select what in a routing? If a work center has multiple capacities you specify the work center in an operation in a routing as any other work center.

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Safety Data Sheet Customization and Generation
entry.content:
Hello

i am pretty sure: this functionality is currently only available via "cloud" version and not on premise version 2023.

Check may be marketplace for more details.

At least the data model in "on premise" 2023 to generate a SDS is not complete. 

C.B.

PS: we above cited link refers to a "cloud" version. Here is the text in help.sap.com. I have highlighted the important part.

"With this process, you can generate a first selection of sections of an EU safety data sheet (without country/region-specific additions) in the SAP S/4HANA for product compliance solution using Adobe Document Services."

entry.title: Re: How do I modify a field of BSEG in FB01? Substitution...
entry.content:
Hi, I have tried to exclude the BSEG-FDTAG in GB01. However, I'm encountering an issue. The ABAP code applied is not working as expected.

Before we have modified the ZOBJECT for Substitution, there BSEG-FDTAG can be modified but I'm not yet sure where the logic for that.

Before the ABAP changes, there is an existing logic for Planning Date but I can't seem to find it in any custom objects.

Example. Payment Terms is 0001, The Days/percent will be set to 100 days. And the formula for Planning Date is Date Today + Days/Percent. So the date will be around November 29, 2024.

 

However, when we tried to use the Payment Terms 0002, the days will be set to 101 days. However, the Planning Date that was seen in FB03 was the Date Today or the Date the document was posted or equal to Baseline Date.

entry.title: Cognos Report Net Delivery with SAP Concur SFTP
entry.content:
SAP Concur can send Intelligence reports to the SFTP account hosted by SAP Concur. This capability is available to all clients with SAP Concur Intelligence and SFTP account with SAP Concur.

How does it work?

The 2 requirements must be met:

  1. Your company must have Intelligence (which provides the Professional Author’s role to allow scheduling of reports).
  2. SAP Concur SFTP Access must exist for the entity. If you have more than one SFTP, please verify which login ID the files should go to.

Once the above conditions are fulfilled, you just need to send reports to your SFTP by emailing the report to <entitycode>@rn.concursolutions.com or <entitycode-NN>@rn.concursolutions.com (if multiple file route keys have been configured and specified). Entity code (it’s Concur internal code, it helps us to identify your company in Concurs system) and the exact email will be given to you by your Project Manager/Technical Consultant/Concur Support.

For more details, please go to https://www.concurtraining.com/customers/tech_pubs/Docs/FactSheets/FTP_Cognos_Reports_Delivery_Fact_Sheet.pdf

 

See the steps below how you can schedule the reports to be delivered to your SFTP account.

Before scheduling a report, keep the following best practices in mind:

  • Avoid scheduling a report with extremely large data sets. It may time out.
  • Be sure the delivery format meets the needs of the recipient(s)
  • Check recipient(s) before finalizing the schedule to ensure only appropriate parties receive the report.
  • Ensure prompt selections are appropriate for a report that will run on a schedule.
  • Include the name of the person who owns the schedule in the body of the email, if possible.
  • Use email address <entitycode>@rn.concursolutions.com under Delivery>Send Report by Email to deliver the report to SFTP server.
  • To send the report to both production and test sftp accounts there should be two instances of the same report using either <prodentitycode>@rn.concursolutions.com or <testentityID>@rn.concursolutions.com under Delivery>Send Report by Email. Using both emails to deliver the report to test and prod sftp in the same schedule can create a conflict and the report would not be delivered to one of the sftp folders.

Schedule a Report

  1. Navigate to the file location of the report to be scheduled. Select the “…” to open the menu and choose properties). Select Schedule > Create Schedule. Note: Standard reports cannot be scheduled for delivery from the schedule reports folder. Copy the standard report to a public folder or a My Content folder prior to creating a schedule.

 

 

 

2.There are 3 tabs on the schedule window. On the schedule tab, review the follow sections:

  1. Frequency:
    1.           Change delivery to weekly, daily, or monthly. To deliver a report every workday, choose “weekly” and select Monday – Friday.
    2.           Change the day(s) of the week or month.
  2. Period:
    1.           Change when delivery should begin or end.
    2.           Change the delivery time. 
  3. Advanced:
    1.           View or update credentials. Credentials uniquely identify the user who scheduled the report.
    2.           Credentials may need to be updated if the previous schedule owner leaves/changes roles.

 

 

  1. On the options tab, review the following sections:
  1. Format – Change the file format of the report as appropriate.
  2. Delivery
    1.           Select “save” to ensure the report runs are saved in Cognos.
    2.           Cognos saves the last 5 runs of the report schedule.
  •           Check the box next to “send report by email” >>use email address <entitycode>@rn.concursolutions.com to deliver the report to SFTP server.
  1. Click “edit details” to update the email message and recipients.

 

 

  1. Once “edit details” is selected, the email screen is displayed. Remove the default email address and enter list of email addresses. Update the subject line and add a message as appropriate.
  2. Scroll down to check “attach report output”. Be sure that tab is blue.

 

 

  1. Prompts – If the report has a prompt page, choose “set values” to open the prompt page. Choose prompt values and click finish (not pictured)

  2.  



    e. The selected prompts are displayed. Choose “save” to set the schedule

     

     

Once the report generation is completed it will be delivered to the out folder of your SFTP server.

entry.title: How to sort the components as BOM sorting when printing production order
entry.content: We know we can sort the components in the component overview in the production order as BOM sorting. But we can't sort as same way when printing. How to?

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Form - Data Binding using Global Data and use of FM FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE
entry.content:
Hi @Chad_He ,

You are correct, Your approach of setting a global field via routine and binding it to multiple fields in the form is practical. But I feel there are specific scenarios where this approach might face challenges compared to using global data binding directly, for instance:

Inter-Form Data Sharing:
Scenario: Scenarios where data needs to be shared between multiple forms or documents.

Issue: Setting global fields manually in one form does not automatically extend to other forms or documents. If your solution requires data sharing between multiple forms, manually setting global fields may not be efficient or feasible. Global data binding is typically scoped to a single form, and sharing data across forms might require a different approach or additional configuration.

Also, like you mentioned using import fields in interface like in case of @Abhijeet the HU_NUMBER we are having restriction on multiple form calling to have same importing parameters. We will have to maintain the consistency when calling multiple forms, but in case of global data binding i believe we are just concerned with the xml layout and the data in layout can be easily called with the FM FM [FP_FUNCTION_XML_INTERFACE] irrespective of the importing parameters.

@Abhijeet Even I am new to the global binding any views from your end would be helpful.

Thanks,
Vishal

Could you please guide on migration part. What is the best way to do migration from legacy system to Subscription Billing? 

Just to mention, legacy system don't have objects like Price Element Specifications, Rate Element, Rate Plan Template etc. 

Please suggest.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to control the default Reverse Date at VL09
entry.content: Yes,you can enhance it in the program:RVV50L09 at the head of form:list_body_aufbauen. The code as follow: ENHANCEMENT 1 ZES_VL09_SCREEN. IF SY-TCODE = 'VL09' AND SY-UCOMM = ''. LOOP AT XLIKPUKWA . XLIKPUKWA-STDAT = XLIKPUKWA-WADAT_IST."冲销日期等于过账日期 MODIFY XLIKPUKWA. ENDLOOP. ENDIF. ENDENHANCEMENT.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
entry.content: @Ali_Chalhoub Can you pls check the links again? for me, it leads to a broken page:

entry.title: Setup icon is invisible in the CPQ
entry.content:
Hey Readers,

I hope you all are doing great 😀

In this article, I will provide information on how to display the setup icon on your CPQ account. Occasionally, even if you are the admin, the setup icon may not be accessible or visible. To prevent this situation, you need to follow a few steps outlined below.

1. Reach out to your fellow admin who has setup access.

2. Please request them to adhere to the following instructions:

       - Navigate to Setup icon

- Navigate to the "users" section and choose "Access rights"

- Under permission group tab, locate the admin name in search tab

- If you choose that name, you will be provided with setup sections located on the right end

       - Choose the setup home option for admin.

I hope this little tip may help you.

Thank you for reading this article!

Stay tuned 😉

By,

Abirami Karthikeyan

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
entry.content: @Ali_Chalhoub can you please provide the right link? Seems broken.

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Hidden Gems – SAP Watch List Screening for Simplified Due Diligence and Compliance
entry.content: Appreciate that screening needs third party content, the same as GTS, but why are you limiting the customer to a single watchlist vendor, unlike GTS that can integrate with several providers. Why was the application built with this restriction, and is there any plans to change it

entry.title: Re: Part 2: Connect to on-premise APIs from SAP Cloud Platform API Management Cloud Foundry Environm
entry.content:
Hi,
I have added the provider with the service collection url. When I push the "discover" button, it takes a while, but it is not possible to retrieve the information during a timeout.

How can we solve this, or how can we set the timeout?
In the S4 system I can see in the performance trace its running almost 30 seconds. 

In destinations its possible to add a property html.timeout, but in this functionality its only possible to add the client id.

Thanks!
BR,
Stefan

entry.title: Re: FTE and Proration examples in Compensation
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec ,

In case of standard FTE and Units per year field below 

Instead of (Pay Range midpoint * FTE), we are getting output as (Pay range midpoint /FTE*units per year)

Is this the correct behavior ?
Can we change it to (Pay Range midpoint * FTE)/Unit per year ?

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: Re: Can we share the Alert notification service instance...
entry.content: Currently, SAP Alert Notification service instances could not be shared accross spaces within a single subaccount.

entry.title: Re: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content:
Hi Hanumath,

I'm not able to answer all your questions, but what I know for sure, based on my hands-on experience, is the following:

1. You can start the process by timer - it is standard SBPA feature. First you have to define your process as having API trigger, then in the Control Tower make instance of your process trigger with scheduled trigger (Control Tower -> Environments -> Triggers -> Add Trigger -> select your already defined API trigger -> select "scheduled" -> set date/time of process triggering

2. You can access your data source by oData from the process. First you have to create a BTP destination to your oData service and then create new "Action", selecting "OData Destinations" as the API source.

3. For accessing and manipulating the Excel file in your scheduled process you should use unattended bot. Unfortunately I have no hands on experience with bot working with Excel files, so can't help with this topic.

entry.title: Re: Error with a loan wage type- Payroll
entry.content: The same error occurred with me, and it has been solved after removing the /LRP from table V_T51P6

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Level Agreement Credit Claim made easy in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hello @AlanSinger 

Sorry, I didn't mention my email address in my first reply.

Please send the information to nathalie.pardini@sap.com

Thanks and regards,

Nathalie

entry.title: Re: Language based Email Triggering from IAS portal
entry.content:
Hi Sonia,

can you also give an example how the script should look like?

Initial we got

{
"sourcePath": "$.locale",
"targetPath": "$.locale",
"optional": true
},

on source and target.

But as you mentioned the ID is "defaultLocale".

If I only change the source path the mail is also sent in english...

{
"sourcePath": "$.defaultLocale",
"targetPath": "$.locale",
"optional": true
},

Do I also have to change the target?

Thank you very much!

Best Michael

entry.title: Re: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content: One more thing: Excel file is the worst option for storing data. I strongly recommend to use a kind of database, like HANA Cloud, with oData interface. Then you can access your data with any kind of application, even built in SAP Build Apps, writing no single line of code. Using a database via oData you can eliminate necessity of bot at all, doing everything either in the SBPA process or Build Apps applications.

entry.title: can we count direct reports by business rules ?
entry.content:
we are trying to identify manager of 

direct reports working in Company A.

those managers can be in company A or different companies within the group.

Unfortunately, the standard filter by company and User with team member size > 0 only works for a subset of the expected scope.

we are missing the managers belonging to a different company than A.

i was wondering if it would be possible to count direct reports from Company A within Business rule.

the idea is to build a custom MDF cust_Manager_A where the count of reports from company A would be a property and the main external code would be the Manager PersonIdExternal code.

if the count reaches zero, it will indicate these manager is no longer managing reports in Company A.

unfortunatly, the function cardinality is unable to count reports with specific criteria on jobinfo.

so i

entry.title: Re: Setup icon is invisible in the CPQ
entry.content:
@abirami_kar

Access Rights are not available by default in all customers CPQ tenants. This should be requested via tickets to get Access rights.

entry.title: Re: Push notifications badge
entry.content: I'm sorry, but I have no experience with push notifications at all, so can't help here. The only thing I would suggest is to open a support ticket as it seems like broken functionality.

entry.title: Billing document header & item line wise in screen (VF01, VF02, VF03 ) level additional fields
entry.content:
Hi All,

This is sukumar,

I want additional fields in screen (vf01, vf02, vf03) header & Item level. i have added VBRK table through append structure next step SE51 screen painter level added additional fields then se80 program(SAPMV60A)60001 screen below your references  but it showing error billing header screen please provide any suggestion & blogs

Its showing subscreen tab in billing document header when click tab it show error.

Regards

Sukumar N

entry.title: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - refining data sources with CAP while keeping a clean core
entry.content:
This blog post is the sixth in a series of posts that cover the connectivity options available for SAP Build Apps to interface with SAP systems. The series will cover connecting SAP Build Apps with the following:

  1. CAP-based services
  2. S/4HANA systems
  3. ABAP systems

The format of this post is similar to a tutorial. However, I'll provide more comprehensive details, tips and the opportunity for you to provide feedback. Based on the feedback we will be able to adjust and enhance this post and future parts of the series, and might even be able to improve the products involved as well. 

Introduction 

Before we get into the details of setting up connections between SAP Build Apps and SAP Systems, I intend to introduce a couple of SAP technology components in each post that are relevant for the use cases covered in the series. 

CAP

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) is a framework of languages, libraries, and tools for building enterprise-grade services and applications. It guides developers along a path of proven best practices and a great wealth of out-of-the-box solutions to recurring tasks.

CAP includes many elements such as data modeling, business services, internationalization, resiliency, security and other enterprise-level application features, allowing developers to focus on the business logic rather than technical implementations. It supports different programming languages including JavaScript and Java and can be used on SAP Cloud Platform and other cloud environments.

The CAP framework features a mix of proven and broadly adopted open-source and SAP technologies, as highlighted in the figure below.

 

Use case F : exposing an external data source through CAP

For this use case, we will route the data source from an S/4 HANA Cloud system through a CAP service developed in SAP Build Code and use it in the application previously created in SAP Build Apps. We will also start using revision control to manage our code (and no-code) changes.

Solution diagram

Like in the previous blog post, we will start with a quick look into what needs to change in our solution overview. In this case, the change is relatively small. I've added a direct connection (in red) from the destination handling the S/4HANA Cloud data sources to the CAP service. Instead of using the Business Partner entity directly from the destination in our UI application, we will route the data through our data service. Everything else remains the same. By routing the data source through our CAP service, we have more control over which properties get exposed, we can change access control, and even add application logic.


Managing source code in SAP Build Code

Before we go ahead and make changes to the CAP service we've created earlier, we should actually start managing our code in a Git repository. This way, we can always roll back changes in case things do not turn out as expected.

For this particular case, I will use a public GitHub account. On your GitHub home page, select [+] > New repository. Select your account as owner and provide a unique repository name. Do not select a repository template, README file, .gitignore or license.

Select "Create repository" to create and open the repository. Select the "Code" button and copy the web URL for cloning.

Switch to SAP Build Code and select the Simplified Git tab. Select Clone Repository and provide the URL you've copied. Enter your GitHub user name and key (note: not your regular password).

Adding External Resources

We will now add one of the S/4HANA data sources we've used in our previous blog posts to our project. Select the [+] button for External Resources on the Storyboard. 

Business Partner

In the Service Center tab that opens, select the service provider "SAP BUSINESS ACCELERATOR HUB", and pick the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition catalog service.

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition catalog service, search for Business Partner.

Select "Business Partner (A2X)". This will trigger an authentication flow with SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Provide your user name (email) and SAP credentials.

You can now browse the entities exposed through the data source and check live data to get an idea of what data to use in your application. Select the "Add to Project" button. The external resource and corresponding code snippets will be added to the project.

Going back to the Storyboard, the external resources will be visible.

If you have not created a BTP Destination pointing to the S/4HANA Public Cloud sandbox system yet, please create it now:

This destination points to the generic API end-point for the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud sandbox system. Accessing data will require adding a service path, which will be configured in the SAP Build Code project.

Back in SAP Build Code, select the data source BusinessPartnerA2X and select "Edit Data Source" in the popup menu. If there is a warning indicating the SAP System data source is empty (Mandatory field), then select the destination you've set up just now and Save this configuration. 

Next, we will use (parts of) this S/4HANA data source in our data service. Select the service in the Storyboard and select "Add Service Entity".

Disable the "Enable draft editing", as we do not intend to change data in these data sources, and besides that, this resource does not support draft editing.

Pick the entity BusinessPartnerA2X.A_Supplier. You will notice a large amount of properties which we actually do not need. Therefore, clear all selected items by selecting the tick mark on <all properties>. Now only select Supplier, SupplierName and VATRegistration. Rename the service entity to MySupplier. 

In the settings of the service entity, you may also tick the option that this service is read-only.

 

Now, we will add references to these new SAP S/4HANA data sources in our data model. Select the Products entity and select "Open in Graphical Modeler".

Select the Product entity and in the side menu, select "Add Relationship".

Drop the pointer anywhere in the empty canvas.

Select Target Entity BusinessPartnerA2X.A_Supplier in a "To-One" association.

The data model will look like this:

The service model will look as follows, including an association from Products to MySupplier, which only exposes 3 properties.

And the Storyboard will now show links from the external resource to the data model and data service:


Deploying the CAP data service

Navigate to the Task Explorer (take note: not the file explorer) and select Deploy Project in the context menu.


We have now updated our data service and are ready to consume this in our UI application.


New version for the UI application in SAP Build Apps

Before we start changing the UI application we have created before in SAP Build Apps, we should store the current version. We can do this by using the VERSIONS feature. Alternatively, you could also export the current project as a zip archive and manage this in your revision control system of choice.

Selecting the VERSIONS button will open a dialog where you can create a snapshot of the current application definition. Versioning starts at 1.0.0 and for subsequent versions you will have the choice to increment this based on patches, minor or major updates.

 


Replacing the data source for the Suppliers list

The current UI application is directly connected to the S/4HANA Public Cloud sandbox instance through a destination. We are going to replace this connection with the newly deployed CAP service. 

Navigate to the DATA tab in SAP Build Apps and remove the integration we've set up for APIHUB-Business-Partner-V2 by selecting Uninstall.

You will be informed that this update affects data bindings:

Open the integration for our CAP service and enable the MySupplier data entity. As you can see, only the fields we've exposed in the CAP service are available here and not the large amount of fields exposed by the S/4HANA system.

Next, we'll need to update the UI application's data bindings that were affected by the removal of the data integration.

On the Supplier overview page, change the configuration of the table to use MySupplier as data source.

And also change the pagination properties:

As we have changed the name of the entity to MySuppliers, we need to update the logic as well. When mounting the page, we now have to get the record collection from MySupplier:

 

When we now run our application, the data on the Supplier overview page is still coming from the S/4HANA Cloud system, but in this case it is routed through our CAP service that we have deployed to the SAP Business Technology Platform. 

We could add Role-based access control for the MySupplier service entity. However, since this SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system only exposes read-only services, and we have also configured the service entity as read-only, we will not make changes here.

As a next step, we will be adding application logic to the CAP service. But I will cover that in the next blog post.

Below is a screenshot of the application running in Preview on my iPhone.

 

Summary – use case F

In this use case we have routed a data source coming from an S/4HANA Cloud 'sandbox' ERP system through a CAP service that we've deployed on SAP Business Technology Platform. By doing so, we are able to control the amount of data properties exposed, and whether the data service entities are draft-enabled and/or read-only. We can also add Role-based access control for each of the data service entities if needed.

We have added our data service's code and configuration settings into a Github repository for revision control. In addition, we've also started using version control for the UI application in SAP Build Apps.

In the UI application developed in SAP Build Apps, we had to update the data bindings to use the new service entity exposed by the CAP service.

What's next

In the next blog post, we will continue on the connectivity topic and add application logic to the CAP service.

entry.title: Re: Dissappearing comments in SAC
entry.content: Hi Lisa Sun, indeed the comment request seems to be present and active.

entry.title: MT940 (SWIFT) Utd.Arab Emir.
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I am making MT940 implementation for United Arab Emirates. Automatic payment is also used in the system. If anyone has done this in the past, can you share with me the assignments made to transaction types?
